channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Yomif Kejelcha broke the 2-hour marathon but got 2nd place. He's still happy
item.description: Ethiopia's Yomif Kejelcha ran the London Marathon in under two hours, but he only got second place. He told NPR he hopes to run his next marathon a minute faster.
item.content:encoded:
Ethiopia's Yomif Kejelcha ran the London Marathon in under two hours, but he only got second place. He told NPR he hopes to run his next marathon a minute faster.

(Image credit: Alex Davidson)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Scientists see Trump's firing of the National Science Board as an attack on research
item.description: The move follows an administration push for cuts to the NSF and raises concerns in the scientific community that it could jeopardize a tradition of independent decisions about federal science grants.
item.content:encoded:
The move follows an administration push for cuts to the NSF and raises concerns in the scientific community that it could jeopardize a tradition of independent decisions about federal science grants.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Linda McMahon punches back at senators questioning Education Department cuts
item.description: In her first appearance on Capitol Hill this year, lawmakers questioned Education Secretary Linda McMahon about students' civil rights and cuts to federal education spending.
item.content:encoded:
In her first appearance on Capitol Hill this year, lawmakers questioned Education Secretary Linda McMahon about students' civil rights and cuts to federal education spending.

(Image credit: Aaron Schwartz)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: With no radical footprint, what drove suspect to try and assassinate Trump?
item.description: An attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner on Saturday has, again, highlighted the climate of political violence in the U.S. But there are still many questions about the motive.
item.content:encoded:
An attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner on Saturday has, again, highlighted the climate of political violence in the U.S. But there are still many questions about the motive.

(Image credit: Andrew Leyden)
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: FCC orders early license renewal for ABC stations following Kimmel's first lady joke
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission has ordered Disney's ABC to seek early broadcast license renewals for the eight TV stations it owns amid backlash over Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Communications Commission has ordered Disney's ABC to seek early broadcast license renewals for the eight TV stations it owns amid backlash over Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Grand jury indicts former FBI director James Comey for a second time
item.description: The case revolves around a photo the former FBI director posted online last year of seashells on a beach arranged to say "8647."
item.content:encoded:
The case revolves around a photo the former FBI director posted online last year of seashells on a beach arranged to say "8647."

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Elon Musk testifies against OpenAI, seeking Sam Altman's ouster
item.description: Musk's lawyers say OpenAI leaders "stole a charity" and Musk warns about the potential dangers of AI: "We don't want to have a Terminator outcome."
item.content:encoded:
Musk's lawyers say OpenAI leaders "stole a charity" and Musk warns about the potential dangers of AI: "We don't want to have a Terminator outcome."

(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Trump's 'American flag blue' reflecting pool project gets a mixed reaction in D.C.
item.description: The pool is being resurfaced in a shade more akin to that of a swimming pool. It's one of many physical changes Trump is planning for the nation's capital.
item.content:encoded:
The pool is being resurfaced in a shade more akin to that of a swimming pool. It's one of many physical changes Trump is planning for the nation's capital.

(Image credit: Rachel Treisman)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: South Korean court extends prison sentence for wife of ousted president
item.description: In January, Kim Keon Hee was sentenced to 20 months for accepting gifts from the Unification Church, which sought political favors.
item.content:encoded:
In January, Kim Keon Hee was sentenced to 20 months for accepting gifts from the Unification Church, which sought political favors.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Natural disasters can cause another crisis for those recovering from opioid addiction
item.description: People recovering from opioid addiction risk relapse when they can't get their medications after natural disasters. A group of doctors is calling for lawmakers to ease access to the meds.
item.content:encoded:
People recovering from opioid addiction risk relapse when they can't get their medications after natural disasters. A group of doctors is calling for lawmakers to ease access to the meds.

(Image credit: JIM WATSON/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Andrew Jones
